records.ety: [OE. twinklen, AS. twinclian; akin to OE. twinken to blink, wink, G. zwinken, zwinkern, and perhaps to E. twitch.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Twinkled (tw"k'ld); p. pr. & vb. n. Twinkling (tw"klng).]

defs: To open and shut the eye rapidly; to blink; to wink.
quotes: The owl fell a moping and twinkling.

defs: To shine with an intermitted or a broken, quavering light; to flash at intervals; to sparkle; to scintillate.
quotes: These stars do not twinkle when viewed through telescopes that have large apertures.
The western sky twinkled with stars.

defs: A closing or opening, or a quick motion, of the eye; a wink or sparkle of the eye.
quotes: Suddenly, with twinkle of her eye, The damsel broke his misintended dart.

defs: A brief flash or gleam, esp. when rapidly repeated.
The time of a wink; a twinkling.
